tagline: Cinderpeak Arcade: free-to-play reel games with neon-soaked, 80s arcade energy. Drop a quarter of imagination, spin for fun, chase nothing but the vibes.
hero_headline: Insert Coin. Feel The Glow. Play Free!
hero_intro: Welcome to Cinderpeak Arcade, where the cabinets never dim and every spin is pure, no-strings fun. Six neon reel games, zero cost, all vibes.
home_lead: Remember when an arcade was a temple of blinking lights, chiptune anthems, and that gorgeous hum of a hundred machines waiting for you? We bottled that feeling and lit it on fire. Cinderpeak Arcade is a free-to-play playground built by people who grew up chasing high scores under buzzing neon tubes. Pull up a stool, crack your knuckles, and let the reels roll.

home_sections.heading: Six Cabinets, One Endless Quarter
home_sections.body:
Every game in the Cinderpeak lineup is a spinner built for the joy of the pull. No clock. No cost. Just you, the reels, and that satisfying clack as symbols line up across the grid. From ancient temples to neon jungles, each machine has its own soundtrack, its own personality, and its own trail of feature rounds waiting to fire.
Think of it like the corner arcade you never wanted to leave, except the doors never close and the change machine is always full of imaginary tokens. Explore all six, find your favourite, and keep coming back for the free rounds.

home_sections.heading: Play For The Fun Of It
home_sections.body:
Let's be crystal clear about the vibe here: Cinderpeak Arcade is entertainment, plain and simple. There is nothing of monetary value on these reels and nothing to be gained but a good time. You are not chasing anything real, you are chasing that little dopamine spark when three glowing icons snap into place.
We built it that way on purpose. Strip away the pressure and what's left is the thing we loved about arcades in the first place: pure, silly, glorious play. Spin because it feels great. Stop whenever you want. Come back whenever the mood strikes.

home_sections.heading: Made By Arcade Kids, For Everyone 21+
home_sections.body:
We are a scrappy independent studio with a serious soft spot for pixel art, chiptune loops, and the smell of a carpeted game room at midnight. Every reel, every symbol animation, every crunchy sound effect got sweated over by a tiny team who genuinely love this stuff.
Cinderpeak Arcade is for grown-ups who never stopped loving the glow. If you are 21 or older and you feel that old pull toward a flashing cabinet, you are exactly who we made this for. Grab a spot, turn up the volume, and let the summit ember burn.

games_intro: Roll up, roll up! Here's the full Cinderpeak lineup, six free-to-play reel games each with its own flavour of neon-drenched chaos. Whether you crave the crumbling grandeur of a lost temple, the venom-fast reflexes of a jungle chase, or the goofy croak of a fire-breathing amphibian, there's a cabinet with your name buzzing above it. Every one is free, every one is built purely for fun, and none of them ask you for a single thing but your attention. Pick a machine, hit spin, and let the reels do their thing. No pressure, no cost, just play.
games_intro_meta: Explore all six free-to-play reel games at Cinderpeak Arcade. Neon spinners built purely for fun, no cost, nothing but good vibes.

aztec-idols.tagline: Descend into the temple where the stone faces spin.
aztec-idols.description: Aztec Idols drags you deep into a moss-choked temple where torchlight flickers across carved stone faces and the reels themselves feel ancient. Every spin is a rumble of rolling glyphs, golden idols, and glowing sun-discs snapping across the grid, all wrapped in a chunky pixel-art style that would have felt right at home in a 1989 cabinet. The soundtrack is all low ceremonial drums and bright chiptune stabs, building tension as the symbols tumble into place. Line up a run of matching idols and the temple wakes up: torches flare, the stone rumbles, and a feature round of tumbling reels kicks off, clearing matched symbols so fresh ones cascade down from above. It is hypnotic in the best arcade way, that lovely loop of anticipation and payoff, minus anything real on the line. Collect enough sun-discs and a free rounds sequence lights the whole temple in electric gold. There is nothing to chase here but the sheer fun of watching an ancient machine come alive under your fingertips. Aztec Idols is pure atmosphere, pure spin, and pure free-to-play joy for the curious explorer in you.
aztec-idols.how_to_play: Choose your spin, then hit the button to roll the reels. Matching idol symbols along a line trigger tumbling reels, where matched icons vanish and new ones drop in. Collect sun-disc symbols to fire the free rounds feature. Everything is free and just for fun, so relax and enjoy the descent.

black-mamba.tagline: Fast, sleek, and coiled to strike across the reels.
black-mamba.description: Black Mamba is all speed and shadow, a reel game slithering with jungle menace and neon-green venom trails. The moment you hit spin, the grid comes alive with hissing serpents, glowing egg clusters, and pulsing tribal masks, every icon rendered in that crisp, high-contrast pixel style that screams late-night arcade. The audio is a heartbeat of hand drums and synth stabs that quicken as the reels lock in. What makes this cabinet sing is its striking speed mechanic: land the mamba symbol and it lunges across the grid, turning adjacent tiles wild-electric and chaining matches into a rush of cascading feature rounds. Collect enough venom symbols and the whole board goes fluorescent for a burst of free rounds where the serpent never stops moving. It is fast, it is punchy, and it gives you nothing but a jolt of pure arcade adrenaline, exactly the way we like it. There is no cost, nothing real on the line, just the thrill of the coil and the strike. If you love a spinner that keeps your reflexes twitching, Black Mamba is your machine. Slide in and feel the venom.
black-mamba.how_to_play: Set your spin and roll the reels. Landing the mamba symbol turns nearby tiles electric, chaining more matches together. Gather venom symbols to unlock the free rounds feature, where the serpent keeps striking across the grid. It is entirely free and purely for fun, so lean into the speed.

blazin-bullfrog.tagline: One goofy amphibian, a mouthful of fire, endless spins.
blazin-bullfrog.description: Blazin' Bullfrog is the arcade at its silliest and most gloriously fun, a swamp-side reel game starring one absurd fire-breathing amphibian with a serious attitude. Spin the reels and watch lily pads, glowing dragonflies, sizzling flies, and our star frog puff across a grid drenched in swampy greens and hot ember oranges. The pixel art is cartoonish and bouncy, the sound design a mix of croaks, sizzles, and bright bubbly chiptune hooks that make you grin every time the reels stop. The signature moment comes when the bullfrog lands centre-grid, inflates like a balloon, and blasts a jet of flame that scorches a whole row of symbols, upgrading them into matching flame tiles for a chain of feature rounds. Collect enough dragonflies and the swamp erupts into a free rounds sequence where the frog belches fire nonstop. It is loud, ridiculous, and impossible not to enjoy, precisely the kind of good-natured nonsense we set out to build. Nothing of value rides on any spin, there is nothing to gain but pure amphibian comedy. Blazin' Bullfrog is a hug from the goofiest corner of the arcade, and it is completely free to play.
blazin-bullfrog.how_to_play: Pick your spin and roll. When the bullfrog lands in the centre it breathes fire across a row, turning those symbols into matching flame tiles for extra matches. Collect dragonflies to trigger the free rounds feature. It is free and just for laughs, so enjoy the croaky chaos.

canine-carnage.tagline: Unleash the pack across a howling neon grid.
canine-carnage.description: Canine Carnage lets the dogs out in the most joyous, chaotic way possible. This reel game bursts with a pack of pixel pups, scrappy strays, sleek hounds, and one enormous top-dog symbol, all bounding across a grid lit up like a midnight dog park under stadium floodlights. The colour palette is electric purple and bark-brown with flashes of hot amber, and the soundtrack yips and barks in cheerful chiptune rhythm as the reels tumble. The headline mechanic is the pack chase: land three or more matching pups and they sprint together, dragging in nearby symbols and triggering a stampede of feature rounds that ripple across the board. Gather enough bone symbols and the whole kennel breaks loose for a free rounds run where the pack never stops running. It is fast, furry, and full of that scrappy arcade energy we adore, a machine that feels like herding a hundred excited puppies through a light show. There is nothing real on the line and nothing to chase but the sheer chaotic fun of the pack. Canine Carnage is free-to-play mayhem with a waggly tail, and it wants you to come play fetch.
canine-carnage.how_to_play: Set your spin and roll the reels. Three or more matching pups start a chase that pulls in nearby symbols for bigger matches. Collect bone symbols to release the free rounds feature. Everything is free and purely for fun, so let the pack run wild.

battle-royal.tagline: Suit up for a regal rumble across the reels.
battle-royal.description: Battle Royal throws you into a gleaming throne-room showdown where knights, crowns, shields, and glowing royal seals clash across a grid dripping with medieval-neon grandeur. Every spin lands with the satisfying clang of a gauntlet, the reels rendered in bold pixel armour and banner-red heraldry that would look magnificent on any 90s cabinet. A driving chiptune fanfare swells as the symbols lock in, giving each roll a sense of grand ceremony. The centrepiece is the duel mechanic: land two crest symbols on opposite ends of the grid and they charge toward each other, transforming every tile in their path into matching royal seals for a cascade of feature rounds. Rally enough banner symbols and the castle gates fling open for a free rounds siege where the heraldry keeps stacking. It has all the pomp and punch of a proper arcade epic, layered with that scrappy handmade charm we bake into everything. Nothing of value is ever at stake here, there is nothing to chase but the theatrical thrill of the clash. Battle Royal is free-to-play pageantry, a spinner that lets you feel like royalty for the sheer fun of it. Take the throne and roll.
battle-royal.how_to_play: Choose your spin and roll the reels. Two crest symbols on opposite sides charge across the grid, turning tiles between them into matching royal seals. Collect banner symbols to launch the free rounds feature. It is completely free and just for fun, so enjoy the royal rumble.

agent-destiny.tagline: Go undercover in a spinner full of neon espionage.
agent-destiny.description: Agent Destiny is a slick, shadowy spy caper crammed into a reel game, all trench coats, laser tripwires, briefcases, and glowing dossier symbols sliding across a grid lit like a rooftop chase at 2am. The pixel art leans into cool blues and electric magenta, the soundtrack a moody synth-noir groove that pulses harder as the reels click into place. This cabinet oozes cinematic cool, every spin feeling like the tense beat before a heist. The signature mechanic is the infiltration sequence: land the agent symbol and they slip across the grid, unlocking locked tiles and revealing hidden matching icons for a chain of feature rounds. Crack enough safe symbols and the vault swings open for a free rounds mission where the agent keeps working the shadows. It is stylish, suspenseful, and endlessly re-spinnable, a machine built for players who like their arcade fun with a splash of intrigue. Nothing real is ever on the line, there is nothing to chase but the pure thrill of the caper. Agent Destiny is free-to-play espionage with a wink and a smirk, and your mission, should you choose to accept it, is simply to have a blast. Roll the reels, agent.
agent-destiny.how_to_play: Set your spin and roll the reels. The agent symbol slips across the grid, unlocking locked tiles to reveal matching icons. Collect safe symbols to trigger the free rounds feature and open the vault. It is entirely free and just for fun, so enjoy the mission.

about.meta: The scrappy indie studio behind Cinderpeak Arcade, built from a lifelong love of classic neon arcades. Meet the team.
about.story:
Cinderpeak Arcade started, like most good things, with a busted old cabinet and a stubborn refusal to let a good feeling die.
We are a tiny independent studio, three humans and a very loud coffee machine, and we are here because none of us ever really left the arcade. We are the kids who spent whole summers in dim, carpeted rooms lit only by the glow of a hundred machines, memorising attract-mode loops, chasing that perfect run, and living for the moment the reels or the pixels or the score counter did exactly what we hoped. That feeling never faded for us. It just got harder to find.
So we decided to build our own corner of it. From scratch. On a shoestring. With way too much love.
Every single thing at Cinderpeak Arcade is handmade by our little crew. We draw the pixel art ourselves, frame by frame. We compose the chiptune loops ourselves, hunched over synths at midnight. We argue for hours about whether a symbol should clack or clunk when it lands, because those details are the whole point. There is no giant corporation behind this, no boardroom, no focus group telling us to sand off the weird edges. Just three people who think a fire-breathing bullfrog is genuinely hilarious and want to share that joy with you. We answer our own emails, we squash our own bugs, and we celebrate every tiny milestone with far too much noise and far too much cake. When you play a Cinderpeak game, you are playing something a real, sleep-deprived, endlessly enthusiastic human sweated over until it felt exactly right.
The Ember Summit theme came from a road trip we took together, watching the sun set over a ridge that glowed like a dying arcade screen. That warm, flickering ember light is the whole mood we chase: cosy, nostalgic, a little bit magic. Cinderpeak is the peak we imagined at the top of that ridge, a summit where the neon never goes dark.
Here is the thing we care about most, and the thing that shapes every decision we make: this is play for the pure joy of play. Our reel games are free, forever, and there is nothing of monetary value anywhere near them. Nothing to gain, nothing to lose, nothing real on the line. We stripped all of that away on purpose, because we wanted to rebuild the thing we actually loved about arcades, the feeling, not the pressure. Just you, the glow, and that little spark of delight when the reels line up.
We are proudly small, proudly independent, and proudly obsessed with getting the fun exactly right. If you feel that old arcade pull, you are one of us. Pull up a stool. The machines are warm and waiting.

team.name: Marco Ferreira
team.bio: Marco is the stubborn heart of the studio, the guy who insisted we hand-tune every reel until it felt just right. He grew up repairing coin-op cabinets in his uncle's back room and still swears the best sound in the world is a machine powering on. When he is not obsessing over symbol animations, he is hunting flea markets for busted arcade parts to revive.

team.name: Lena Sundqvist
team.bio: Lena draws every glowing icon, every flame, every goofy frog you see, all by hand, pixel by loving pixel. Her style fuses 80s neon with a warm ember glow that gives Cinderpeak its whole look. She is happiest with a stylus in hand and a chiptune album blasting, chasing the exact shade of purple a midnight arcade should be.

team.name: Ravi Chandra
team.bio: Ravi builds the guts of every game and composes the crunchy chiptune loops that make the reels feel alive. A self-taught coder and lifelong synth tinkerer, he treats sound design like a science and an art at once. He will happily spend a full day arguing that a symbol should clack in exactly the key of E, and honestly, he is usually right.

contact.meta: Get in touch with the Cinderpeak Arcade crew. Questions, high-five stories, or just want to say hi? We are all ears.
contact.intro:
Got a question, a bug to squash, or just want to tell us which cabinet stole your heart? Drop us a line. We are a tiny crew, so a real human on our team reads every message, and we genuinely love hearing from players.
Whether you spotted a wonky pixel, have an idea for a symbol, or simply want to swap arcade memories, our inbox is always open. Do not be shy, we built this whole place to talk to people like you.
contact.hours: Mon–Fri, 9am–6pm (we answer as fast as three humans can)
contact.community: Join the glow in our community channels for chiptune drops, sneak peeks, and player high-scores chatter.

privacy:
Cinderpeak Arcade Privacy Policy
We are a small independent studio and we treat your privacy the way we would want ours treated: carefully, honestly, and with as little fuss as possible. This policy explains what we collect, why, and what you can do about it. It is written in plain English because legalese gives us a headache too.
What we collect. When you play our free reel games, we may collect basic technical information automatically, such as your browser type, device type, general location at the country or region level, and the pages you visit on our site. We do not ask you to create an account to play, and we do not collect your name, address, or any sensitive personal details unless you choose to give them to us, for example when you email our team.
Cookies and analytics. We use a small number of cookies and similar technologies to help the site work properly and to understand how players move around it. Some cookies are essential for the site to function. Others are analytics cookies that give us anonymous, aggregated data about how many people visit and which games they enjoy most. This helps us make better games. You can disable cookies in your browser settings at any time, though some features may not work as smoothly if you do.
How we use your data. We use the information we collect only to keep the site running, fix problems, improve our games, and respond to messages you send us. We do not sell your personal data to anyone, ever. We do not build advertising profiles about you.
Third parties. We may use trusted third-party services, such as web analytics and hosting providers, that process limited data on our behalf. These providers are bound to protect your information and use it only for the services they provide to us.
Your choices. You can browse and play without giving us any personal information. If you have emailed us and want us to delete your correspondence, just ask and we will. You can also clear or block cookies through your browser at any time.
Contact. If you have any questions about privacy or want to make a request about your data, email us at hello@cinderpeakarcade.example and a real person will get back to you. We may update this policy from time to time, and any changes will be posted on this page with a fresh date.
terms:
Cinderpeak Arcade Terms of Use
Welcome, and thanks for playing. By using Cinderpeak Arcade you agree to these terms. We have kept them short and readable because we would rather you spent your time playing than deciphering fine print.
Free to play, always. All of our reel games are completely free to play. There is no charge to spin, ever. We built this place for the pure joy of the arcade, not for anything else.
Entertainment only, nothing of value. This is the most important thing to understand. Cinderpeak Arcade is entertainment and nothing more. There is nothing of monetary value anywhere in our games, nothing real is ever at stake, and there is nothing of value to be gained from playing. Any tokens, symbols, or in-game elements exist purely for fun and have no value outside the game and cannot be exchanged for anything. Please treat our games as the light-hearted, no-stakes entertainment they are.
Age requirement. You must be 21 years of age or older to use this site. By playing, you confirm that you meet this requirement. If you do not, please do not use the site.
Acceptable use. Please play fairly and kindly. Do not attempt to hack, disrupt, reverse-engineer, or overload the site, do not use bots or automated tools to access it, and do not use the site for any unlawful purpose. Be respectful when you contact our team. We reserve the right to restrict access to anyone who abuses the site or these terms.
Intellectual property. All the art, audio, code, and content on Cinderpeak Arcade is created by our studio and belongs to us. You are welcome to enjoy it here, but please do not copy, redistribute, or resell any of it without our written permission.
Liability. We work hard to keep the site running smoothly, but we provide it on an as-is basis. We cannot guarantee it will always be available or error-free, and to the fullest extent permitted by law we are not liable for any loss arising from your use of the site. Nothing here is professional advice of any kind.
Changes. We may update these terms from time to time as our little studio grows and our games evolve. When we do, we will post the updated terms on this page. Continuing to use the site after changes means you accept the new terms.
Questions? Email us at hello@cinderpeakarcade.example. Now go enjoy the glow.

testimonials.name: Remi H.
testimonials.text: I honestly forgot how good it feels to just play something with zero pressure. The fire-breathing frog cabinet had me grinning like an idiot at midnight. The neon look and the crunchy little sound effects nail that old arcade feeling perfectly. I keep coming back just to hear the reels clack.

testimonials.name: Noor K.
testimonials.text: The temple game is my absolute favourite, those tumbling reels are so satisfying to watch. It is clearly made by people who actually love arcades, you can feel the care in every pixel. No cost, no catch, just pure fun. Exactly what I needed after a long day.

testimonials.name: Teo M.
testimonials.text: The spy-themed spinner is dripping with atmosphere, the synth soundtrack alone is worth showing up for. I love that it is free and there is genuinely nothing on the line, so I can just relax and enjoy the vibe. It scratches that retro arcade itch better than anything else I have found.
